pi_items.source: Verified as real dataset from Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5), containing US quarterly macroeconomic data from Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED)
Verified in setup_task.sh line 12 and export_result.sh line 7
Mathematical verification: 2008 - 1984 + 1 = 25 years. Also verified in task description: 'Convert the quarterly US GDP dataset...1984-2008'
Task description: 'The file must contain at least gdp, inf, and gdp_growth'. Verifier checks for these exact columns.
USA.gdt dataset in install_gretl.sh specifies frequency='4' (quarterly). Data from 1984:1 to 2009:3 = 103 quarterly observations.
Task description step 3: 'Compact the data to Annual frequency'
Task description step 3: 'Compact the data to Annual frequency using the Average method (since the source is already a rate)'

pi_items.verified_value: Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) - specifically Real GDP (chained 2005 dollars, seasonally adjusted) and CPI inflation rate
pi_items.source: Install_gretl.sh comments (lines 125-138): 'Source: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database'. USA.gdt XML description specifies 'gdp = Real Gross Domestic Product (billions of chained 2005 dollars, seasonally adjusted annual rate)' and 'inf = CPI inflation rate (quarterly % change, annualized)'

Embedded quarterly data in install_gretl.sh (lines 292-295): 1984 Q1-Q4 = [7285.0, 7340.9, 7412.7, 7493.0]; Average = (7285.0 + 7340.9 + 7412.7 + 7493.0) / 4 = 7382.9. NOTE: Verifier value (6940.6) does not match embedded data and cannot be found in public FRED databases.

Embedded quarterly data in install_gretl.sh (lines 296-299): 1985 Q1-Q4 = [7574.1, 7648.1, 7703.2, 7777.0]; Average = (7574.1 + 7648.1 + 7703.2 + 7777.0) / 4 = 7675.6. NOTE: Verifier value (7354.6) does not match embedded data.

Embedded quarterly data in install_gretl.sh (lines 380-383): 2008 Q1-Q4 = [14530.3, 14598.9, 14665.0, 14706.3]; Average = (14530.3 + 14598.9 + 14665.0 + 14706.3) / 4 = 14625.1. NOTE: Verifier value (13312.8) does not match embedded data.

Install_gretl.sh explicitly downloads POE5 data from real economic sources (FRED for Real GDP via BEA, CPIAUCSL for inflation via BLS). The dataset comes from Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis and is NOT synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: The usa.gdt dataset is a real, verified dataset from Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition containing US quarterly macroeconomic data from Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED). The dataset has 103 quarterly observations from 1984 Q1 to 2009 Q3. Converting to annual frequency (1984-2008) produces exactly 25 rows as claimed. The source variables are Real GDP (in billions of chained 2005 dollars, seasonally adjusted) and CPI inflation rate (annualized quarterly percentage). The task requires aggregating quarterly data to annual using the Average method and calculating GDP growth using the log-difference formula 100*(ln(gdp)-ln(gdp(-1))). The expected output columns are gdp, inf, and gdp_growth. CRITICAL ISSUE: The verifier contains hardcoded ground truth values (1984: 6940.6, 1985: 7354.6, 2008: 13312.8) that contradict the embedded dataset values in install_gretl.sh (1984: 7382.9, 1985: 7675.6, 2008: 14625.1). These verifier values cannot be verified against public FRED databases and may represent a different version of the dataset or incorrect values.
